summary: apply_cleanups.py: make re-apply idempotent by TOML state, not applied_at (rebuild-proof accepted rewrites)
description: From-zero rebuild gap found while answering 'would we get these bad strings again?': a re-chunk regenerates hard-wrapped bodies, and accepted staged_cleanups rows survive in guru.db with original_body matching the fresh TOMLs — but apply_cleanups.py filters applied_at IS NULL, so previously-applied rewrites silently do not re-apply after a rebuild. Fix: decide applicability from TOML state (TOML==original_body AND status=accepted -> applicable; TOML==proposed_body -> already applied; anything else -> stale refusal) and treat applied_at as an audit stamp only. Idempotent by construction. Note the regex half (clean_bodies P1-P9) is already rebuild-safe: deterministic script + round-trip test enforces stored==clean_body(raw).
